channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Coup dur pour Denuvo : à peine sorti, Resident Evil Requiem est le premier jeu de 2026 à être cracké
item.description: Le logiciel anti-piratage Denuvo, véritable bête noire des joueurs PC, vient de subir un revers historique. À peine 41 jours après sa sortie mondiale, Resident Evil Requiem a été totalement débarrassé de ses verrous numériques. Les protections de nouvelle génération ne sont plus infaillibles.

item.title: NASA used a 12-year-old GoPro to capture a sight called the ‘greatest gift’ by Artemis II pilot — and it used one extreme setting to get the shot
item.description: The Artemis II has provided iconic images throughout its moon fly-by mission, using a string of surprising cameras

item.title: iPhone owners urged to change this key privacy setting after FBI recovers suspect’s deleted Signal messages
item.description: Potential security flaws in both Signal and WhatsApp have been revealed, but one of them is fixable.

item.title: Interior design at 25,000 mph
item.description: As the Artemis II astronauts prepare for the most dramatic and potentially dangerous part of their mission - reentry into Earth's atmosphere - the eyes of the world will be on the Orion capsule and the people inside it. Getting glimpses into the capsule during the mission, the public has been able to observe the […]

item.title: Pour défendre l’iPhone, Apple fait tout pour obtenir les secrets de Samsung
item.description: Apple demande à son principal rival de lui fournir les preuves que les utilisateurs changent librement de smartphone. Le procès antitrust américain prend un tour inattendu.

item.title: Stop the presses — Microsoft is actually cutting cloud PC prices for SMBs, promises to make it 'more cost-effective for small and medium businesses'
item.description: Microsoft is cutting the price of its Cloud PC subscriptions by 20% by reducing performance, but the trade-offs are minimal.

item.title: Return to ‘Tron: Legacy’ With Hasbro’s Electric New Action Figures (Exclusive)
item.description: After trying to bring some classic 'Tron' figures to life, Hasbro is heading back to the grid for a spin with the stars of the 2010 cult classic.

item.title: Microsoft has begun stripping out AI from Windows 11 — but it's already being criticized for not going far enough
item.description: Notepad change shows a rebrand of AI tools, not a removal, and it has skeptics worried.

item.title: Euphoria season 3 full release schedule: what is the launch date and time for episode 1 on HBO Max?
item.description: Here's when and where you'll be able to watch every episode of Euphoria season 3.

item.title: How to read Murder in Purple and Gold online from anywhere
item.description: All the ways to read Murder in Purple and Gold online from anywhere, the 14th entry in Lindsey Davis' beloved Flavia Albia series of historical crime novels.

item.title: Steam cleaners sold at Walmart and Target recalled after 161 people suffered burn injuries
item.description: The Bissell Homecare appliances, sold over a period of roughly a year and a half, can unexpectedly expel hot water due to a faulty attachment. Steam cleaning should be a low-risk activity, but that hasn’t been the case for some owners of Bissell’s Steam Shot products.

item.title: « Je sais maintenant que Jennifer Aniston peut tester la résistance d’une infrastructure » : ce GIF de Friends occupait 337 Go
item.description: Un GIF très populaire de Friends a permis aux développeurs de Discourse de découvrir les limites d'une fonction de Linux.

item.title: Microsoft affirme qu’un « bug » d’horloge de Windows 11 est en réalité volontaire
item.description: Un « bug » de l'écran de verrouillage sécurisé de Windows n'en est finalement pas un selon Microsoft.

item.title: Garmin's cashing in on the screenless Whoop-style smart band trend with its upcoming CIRQA — here's the proof
item.description: Garmin's CIRQA smart band is on the way — here's the US trademark office filing

item.title: These 1970s Predictions for Inflation in 2026 Show Things Could Be a Lot Worse
item.description: For one, they predicted TVs would cost $11,200.

item.title: Cdiscount casse déjà le prix du nouveau MacBook Neo : une offre à ne pas manquer
item.description: Le nouveau MacBook Neo est une belle façon de découvrir l’univers Apple sans exploser son budget. Et en ce moment, c’est encore plus intéressant : grâce à un code promo, Cdiscount enlève 50 € sur son prix, ce qui en fait une très bonne affaire.

item.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman's tenure at OpenAI has been… messy. Messy to the point where Altman was briefly fired from his role as CEO, only to be reinstated days later, at which point he began reshaping the organization permanently. This week, The New Yorker published a deep look at Altman, his time at OpenAI, and the questions […]

item.title: Stockage saturé sur Android ? Cette nouvelle fonction va vous changer la vie
item.description: Android devrait vous faciliter la vie en cas de stockage surchargé avec une fonctionnalité de sauvegarde automatique vers un PC.

item.title: YouTube insists that a 90-sec, unskippable ad format 'isn't something we are testing' — but furious users say 'they very much exist'
item.description: YouTube TV users are complaining about longer ads that can't be skipped, while YouTube says nothing has changed.

item.title: Pour gagner la « bataille de la cybersécurité », la CIA va déployer des « collègues » IA aux côtés de ses agents
item.description: Le directeur adjoint de la CIA explique comment l’intelligence artificielle va se développer au sein du service de renseignement. Dans un contexte particulièrement tendu, la maîtrise de l’IA est primordiale pour la CIA.

item.title: Pixel 11 : Google va chiper le meilleur écran de Samsung avant même Apple
item.description: Les futurs fleurons de Google pourraient bien frapper un grand coup du côté de leur affichage. Selon de récentes fuites, la gamme Pixel 11 devrait être la toute première à inaugurer la nouvelle génération d'écrans OLED de Samsung, coiffant au poteau son éternel rival Apple, et même le propre créateur de la dalle.

item.title: ‘Everything is magenta’: This wild hack got Mac OS X Cheetah working on a Nintendo Wii, and I can’t quite believe it
item.description: A programmer got Mac OS X Cheetah working on a Nintendo Wii, solving myriad problems along the way.

item.title: 'If one piece of your supply chain is delayed, then your whole project can't deliver': Nearly half of US data centers planned for 2026 canceled or delayed — and things could soon get much worse
item.description: Data centers are being delayed or cancelled altogether, and the number of challenges operators face just continues to grow.

item.title: Apple is closing stores in 3 states, joins list of retailers to shutter locations in challenging environment for malls
item.description: Set to close this summer, the Apple Stores include the tech giant’s first unionized location. Here’s what to know. There has been no shortage of retailers closing locations over the last few years as consumer behaviors shift online and foot traffic at brick-and-mortar stores continues to decline for many chains.

item.title: Microsoft starts removing unnecessary Copilot buttons in Windows 11
item.description: Microsoft has rolled out a Notepad update for Windows Insiders that removes the Copilot branding and icon from within the app, Windows Central has reported. The old Copilot menu has been replaced with “writing tools,” but it’s worth noting that the tools are still powered by AI and are pretty much identical to the selection found in the old menu. Microsoft has just replaced the Copilot button with a pen icon. In addition, the company has removed mentions of AI in the Settings menu and has placed the option to disable the AI-powered writing tools within the “Advanced features” section. The comp...

item.title: A new free-to-play Borderlands game gets surprise drop on mobile, which Zynga says is part of a 'limited-time test'
item.description: Out of nowhere and with little fanfare, Zynga has launched a brand new free-to-play Borderlands game for mobile.

item.title: Coachella 2026: How and when to stream the concert event live
item.description: California’s popular music bonanza begins this weekend with its biggest livestream yet. No fewer than 7 stages will be available to watch. For the next two weekends (April 10-12 and April 17-19), Los Angeles is going to be quieter than normal.

item.title: Maul’s Lightsabers in ‘Shadow Lord’ Are Powered by Sam Witwer’s Screams
item.description: One of the most beloved angsty men of 'Star Wars' has found a new way to have edge.

item.title: Snap is sticking with Qualcomm for its next AR glasses
item.description: Snap's upcoming consumer AR glasses are still set to launch sometime this year, and they'll be powered by a Snapdragon XR chip from Qualcomm, according to an announcement of a "multi-year strategic agreement" between the companies. Snap has been working on smart glasses for a decade, with the first model of its camera-equipped Spectacles glasses […]...

item.title: Snap gets closer to releasing new AI glasses after years-long hiatus
item.description: Specs has long been teasing the next version of its augmented reality glasses. A new partnership with Qualcomm promises some movement in that department.

item.title: I spent a week testing the Xiaomi 17, and it outmuscles the iPhone 17 and Galaxy S26 in several key areas
item.description: The Xiaomi 17 is a feature-packed flagship with a smaller footprint.

item.title: The future of work is here, but hiring hasn’t caught up
item.description: The problem isn’t talent—it’s how companies define and find it. Companies today are facing a paradox they can’t seem to solve: Roles are going unfilled while millions of capable workers remain overlooked.

item.title: AI writing is the technology’s bleakest use case
item.description: In writing, there’s an old adage: Write what you know. This, of course, requires knowing something in the first place. Will artificial intelligence enable an even higher level of creativity, or turn us into cognitively deflated Sims spitting out chatbot responses?

item.title: ChatGPT’s hidden backup model just got smarter — as OpenAI adds a cheaper Pro option
item.description: OpenAI has upgraded ChatGPT’s hidden fallback model while rolling out a new $100 Pro tier that makes its pricing, and who gets what, more layered than ever

item.title: Northsocial.ca, un nouveau réseau social canadien
item.description: Un groupe d’anciens étudiants de l’Université de la vallée du Fraser, en Colombie-Britannique, a lancé un nouveau réseau social canadien qui, selon eux, offrira une « alternative locale aux sites dominés par les États-Unis ».

item.title: Forget Big Mistakes — this jaw-dropping new Netflix true crime series is the only TV show you need to stream this weekend
item.description: While everybody is talking about Big Mistakes on Netflix, the streamer has quietly released its greatest true crime docuseries of all time.

item.title: Artemis II reentry and the risks of ‘riding a fireball through the atmosphere’
item.description: After a successful lunar loop, the four astronauts face one more hurdle to splashdown, and it’s the most dangerous. After glorious lunar views, a moving dedication, a malfunctioning toilet, and a floating Nutella, Artemis II is poised for the riskiest part of its 10-day journey to the far side of the moon.

item.title: Aventon Current ADV Electric Mountain Bike Review: Feels Just Like the Real Thing
item.description: Aventon’s latest electric mountain bike lets you bomb hills while avoiding the part where you get sweaty by pedaling back to the top.

item.title: Anthropic’s ‘Mythos’ AI proves that obsessing over AGI is folly
item.description: Maybe LLMs will beat humanity at everything someday. But they’re adept at exploiting software vulnerabilities right now—and we can’t wait for fixes. Hello again, and welcome back to Fast Company’s Plugged In.

item.title: 'The problem is not AI’s capability...what won’t improve on its own is the human side': Major study claims white-collar workers are fighting back against AI in the workplace
item.description: New report shows investment in AI isn't enough – companies need to focus on clear guidance and simplified adoption.

item.title: Gen Z’s love-hate relationship with AI
item.description: Gen Z is increasingly disillusioned with AI - just not enough to stop using it. A new Gallup report released this week, based on responses from nearly 1,600 people ages 14 to 29 across the US, suggests the hype is wearing off for the digital-native generation as AI becomes more embedded in school and work. […]

item.title: YouTube Premium is getting pricier
item.description: YouTube Premium is getting more expensive in the US, with prices rising by $2 on standard individual accounts and as much as $4 for the family plan. The price hike is already in effect for new accounts, and is rolling out now for existing customers. Premium's Individual plan now costs $15.99 per month, up from […]
